metrics.narrative: The business pain is well articulated (manual SAP/eBa and Outlook-driven procurement across Middle East, Africa, Turkey, Egypt and future India markets, per the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call), but no quantified financial impact or ROI has been built. Zycus committed on 6 Apr 2026 to develop a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) alongside the project lead and CPO at a face-to-face session targeted for May 2026; that session never took place, so metrics remain unquantified as of 9 Jul 2026.

champion.narrative: Vinoth Kumar initiated the original outreach, attended the discovery call, and articulated requirements clearly, and Salonee Karnik operationally drove scheduling and was set to manage the RFQ process; both read as genuine internal drivers as of early Jan 2026. However, there is no confirmed contact with either since the buyer's last confirmed email reply on 13 Jan 2026 (177 days ago), the planned May 2026 face-to-face session never happened, and Vinoth Kumar is coded in Salesforce as a partner-side contact role rather than a direct Nissan buyer-side role, which raises an unresolved question about the true nature of his relationship to the account. Champion strength is now unverified and at risk.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is logged directly on the opportunity's Competitors field, though no call to date names it as an active rival being evaluated. The real, evidenced competitive dynamic is the buyer's existing SAP plus eBa plus Outlook manual stack as the do-nothing baseline, and a named capability gap: Nissan uses Beroe for market intelligence in other regions and Zycus has no confirmed equivalent, a gap flagged on 8 Jan 2026 and still unresolved. The largest unresolved competitive risk is structural rather than a named vendor: Nissan's EMEA headquarters may mandate a platform already used elsewhere in the region (raised 6 Apr 2026), which could bypass this local evaluation entirely.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly and specifically articulated: no RFQ capability in the current eBa tool, manual processes across procurement, heavy reliance on Outlook for all procurement activity, manual monthly market-intelligence research performed by the EU team, and poor spend visibility with no end-to-end digital procurement. This was laid out directly by the buyer's team on the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call.

paper_process.narrative: No commercial paper, contracting, or legal activity exists on this deal. The opportunity has not progressed past a single discovery call and a caveated budgetary estimate; there is no evidence of an MSA, order form, or SOW conversation at this stage, which is expected this early but leaves paper process entirely unaddressed.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-06-04) states the economic buyer is not explicitly identified. Vinoth Kumar heads procurement for the Middle East/Europe region and is the main internal driver, and intends to invite his 'process team' globally to the demo, implying broader sign-off is needed. Compounding this, the 6 Apr 2026 next-step note raises that Nissan's EMEA headquarters 'may' insist on a solution already used elsewhere in the region, and it is not clear who controls that decision, meaning true budget/vendor-selection authority is unmapped.

decision_process.narrative: Vinoth Kumar described the 7 Jan 2026 session as a 'get-to-know' call ahead of a formal RFQ; the outlined path is pre-RFQ discovery, internal stakeholder alignment, formal RFQ, evaluation, then vendor selection, with Salonee Karnik set to lead the RFQ. An original go-live target of 1 Apr 2026 (with an H2 2026 fallback) was named on the call, contingent on RFP completion and technical/security validation. None of these steps (RFQ launch, demo, validations) have occurred as of 9 Jul 2026, so the process has stalled before its first formal gate.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented and corroborated by the call: end-to-end automation of the procure-to-invoice process, AI-driven sourcing and integrated market intelligence (a named differentiator ask), replacement of Outlook/email-based procurement, SAP HANA integration, and a supplier portal for invoice submission against purchase orders (a buyer question raised directly on the 7 Jan 2026 call).

items.name: New Zycus POC introduction & restart request
items.summary: Mohamad Alhakim (Zycus RVP Middle East) emails Vinoth and Salonee to introduce himself as the new primary contact replacing Dan Quinn, referencing the earlier-year discussions on sourcing/contracting/invoicing priorities across ME, Africa, Turkey and Egypt, and proposes a 20-minute call the week of July 13 to reassess where things stand — reply pending from Nissan.

day_summary.overall: After months of dormancy since the January demo scheduling thread stalled, Zycus re-engaged with a new regional VP, Mohamad Alhakim, taking over as primary contact after Dan Quinn's departure. Alhakim's email to Vinoth and Salonee is essentially a relationship-continuity play — signaling he's briefed on the ME/Africa/Turkey/Egypt procurement transformation agenda and asking to restart the conversation with a 20-minute call the week of July 13. The ball is now in Nissan's court to confirm availability; nothing substantive on scope or commercials has moved yet.

cro_panel.intro: A well-documented early pain and requirements set from a single strong discovery call sits against six months of stalled progress, an unconfirmed economic buyer, and an unresolved regional platform-mandate risk.; The deal is honestly reflected as Pipeline, not further along

Deal has already been disrupted once by external geopolitical events (war pause) and the recovery attempt (May restart) failed to materialize — showing fragility to further stall.
Sole named champion uncontacted for 6 months, with no secondary buyer-side relationship evidenced beyond Salonee Karnik in an operational-support role.

deal_movement.summary: The deal opened strong with a well-received discovery call and budgetary discussion in early January 2026, then stalled through a regional conflict pause, missed its planned May 2026 restart, and is only now being re-approached by the owner in July 2026.

forecast_read.math: Created 2026-01-07, still Qualified as of 2026-07-09 (~183 days in stage). Close date 2026-08-31 is 53 days from today. No demo, RFQ, or ROI workshop has occurred despite these being named as the immediate next steps since Jan 2026.
forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the appropriate, defensible category: there has been no confirmed two-way buyer contact since 13 Jan 2026 (177 days), no demo has been delivered, and no RFQ/RFP has started. The recorded close date of 31 Aug 2026 (53 days out) is not credible given the deal has not progressed past a single discovery call and should be treated as a placeholder pending a forcing event.

critical_signals.text: Nissan's EMEA regional headquarters may require a platform already used elsewhere in the region rather than leaving the choice to the local Middle East team, a risk first raised on 6 Apr 2026 (about 13 weeks ago) and still unresolved as of 9 Jul 2026.

The only substantive session held so far, a discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 (about 6 months ago), went well and produced a clear requirements list, but the follow-on live demo and ROI workshop planned for early-to-mid May 2026 never took place and no new date has been set.
Nissan uses a market-intelligence tool in other regions that Zycus has no confirmed equivalent for; this gap was flagged on 8 Jan 2026 and remains unresolved as of 9 Jul 2026.
Nissan has internal budget secured but is withholding its target price until it sees a formal Zycus pricing proposal, a stance unchanged since 20 Jan 2026 with no update since.

champion_strength.summary: Vinoth Kumar drove the original inbound interest and articulated requirements clearly on the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call, with Salonee Karnik operationally supporting, but there has been no confirmed contact with either since 13 Jan 2026, the planned May 2026 restart never happened, and Vinoth's Salesforce contact-role coding as partner-side (rather than direct Nissan buyer-side) adds ambiguity about the relationship.

expansion_context.note: Cited by Zycus as an existing relationship and reference point during the 2026-01-07 discovery call and in MEDDPICC differentiation notes, but this is a separate regional Salesforce account (Nissan Africa, Middle East & India) from the Australia/US relationship, so it provides credibility leverage rather than direct executive access within this account.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is stalled awaiting a buyer response rather than confirmed dead; the entire qualitative narrative rests on a single strong discovery call from six months ago.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-01-07, 181 days in Qualified as of 7 Jul 2026 with no stage movement. Close date 2026-08-31 is 55 days out. Last content-rich buyer touch was the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call; the last genuine two-way buyer contact (an inbound email reply) was 2026-01-13 (175 days ago). The only newer activity is a rep-initiated re-engagement email on 2026-07-03 (4 days ago), still awaiting reply. No demo, RFQ, or economic-buyer conversation has happened. Reaching a decision in the remaining 55 days from Qualified with none of these steps underway is not realistic.

north_star_verdict.headline: A promising discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 stalled for six months when the regional conflict paused the project; a re-engagement email went out 3 Jul 2026 but the buyer has not yet replied, and the 31 Aug 2026 close date is not achievable on current pace.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Omitted / push close date - no EB confirmed, no demo held, no RFQ launched, and buyer has been silent for 6 months

The real, evidenced threats are the incumbent do-nothing baseline (existing SAP plus eBa plus Outlook processes) and an unresolved market-intelligence capability gap versus Beroe, alongside a structural risk that Nissan's EMEA headquarters could mandate an alternate platform outright.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-documented early pain and requirements set from a single strong discovery call sits against six months of stalled progress, an unconfirmed economic buyer, and an unresolved regional platform-mandate risk. The deal is honestly reflected as Pipeline, not further along.

deal_risk.text: The 2026-08-31 close date (53 days out) is not achievable given the deal has not advanced past a single discovery call; it should be treated as a placeholder pending a real forcing event.
The EMEA headquarters platform-mandate risk (raised 2026-04-06) remains unresolved and unaddressed by any confirmed regional relationship.
The market-intelligence/Beroe capability gap (raised 2026-01-08) is still open, and its resolution (a Smart Cube integration story) has not been built.

win_position.text: Pain and decision criteria are well documented from the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call: manual eBa and Outlook-based procurement, no RFQ capability, and poor spend visibility, giving Zycus a clear opening story.

No economic buyer is confirmed (MEDDPICC 2.0, updated 2026-06-04), and Nissan's EMEA headquarters may mandate a platform used elsewhere in the region (raised 2026-04-06), leaving true vendor-selection authority unmapped.
There has been no confirmed two-way buyer contact since 2026-01-13 (177 days), and the deal remains pre-demo and pre-RFQ, well short of the validation needed for a stronger read at Qualified stage.

The live custom demo and ROI workshop targeted for early-to-mid May 2026 (after the regional ceasefire) never occurred, and no replacement date is scheduled as of 2026-07-09.
The opportunity has sat in Qualified for roughly 183 days since its 2026-01-07 creation with no progression toward Formal Evaluation.

Nissan has not confirmed a target price or reconfirmed intent since around 2026-01-20, and no reply followed the owner's 2026-07-03 outreach as of this sweep.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI differentiation (native Merlin/ANA, autonomous negotiation) was named in MEDDPICC notes as a key differentiator, but has never been shown to the buyer in a live demo, and the specific AI-adjacent ask the buyer raised (a Beroe-equivalent market-intelligence capability) remains an open, unresolved gap since 8 Jan 2026.

items.quote: Key requirements include full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes
items.criterion: End-to-end automation of the procure-to-invoice process

items.quote: only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions
items.criterion: AI-driven sourcing and integrated market intelligence

items.quote: Does the supplier will have a portal where they go and upload their invoice against each purchase order?
items.source: Avoma discovery call notes, 2026-01-07 (buyer question, no confirmed answer captured)
items.criterion: Supplier portal for invoice submission against purchase orders

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits most of the stated criteria on paper, but the one differentiated ask (market intelligence/AI) has an open gap, and none of the fit has been validated with the buyer since the single January 2026 discovery call.

hard.ais_why:
• On Jan 7 2026, Vinoth Kumar (Nissan ME, Head of Procurement) said in the discovery call (note from Dan Quinn): 'Key requirements include full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence.' He continued: 'the system will give me an indication that based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ for this particular sourcing so that you can benefit out of the new technology or the cost.' Buyer explicitly naming AI-driven sourcing as a mandatory requirement.
• Per the 6 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Have paused the project due to the war... EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s)... next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis.' Vinoth wants a live custom demo built on his own process flow - signaling deep solution-led evaluation.
• High confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (nissan-me.ae domain), Jan 7 transcript with 114 notes including direct Vinoth quotes naming AI-driven sourcing and market intelligence integration. Product scope includes Merlin + Merlin Intake + Agentic AI + ANA. Project is paused due to war but the AI vision was articulated by the buyer pre-pause - AI Hungry stands.
hard.next_step: Confidence 10%3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode, <span style="background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); font-size: 14.6667px; color: rgb(36, 36, 36);">email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity 6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May.28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip.20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates.9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap.18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan.17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec
